english: perfection, that is known to Buddhism; there is, however, an essential difference between it and the miracles of holy writ, the former bearing the stamp of non-reality and mere appearance, as is not only implied by the name, but also universally acknowledged; and it differs again from čo-_oprúl (cho 'phrul), in as much as the latter requires the help of natural magic (jugglery), or of demoniacal influences, and never can be produced, like rdzu-_oprúl (rdzu 'phrul), at the pleasure of the saint by his own immanent power. Yet there is no doubt that the term čo-_oprúl (cho 'phrul) is also often used in connection with rdzu-_oprúl (rdzu 'phrul), and as identical with it; v. Dzl. 25 and sqq.

rdzun (rdzun), C. *dzun* (dzun), W. *zun* (zun), Pur. *rdzun* (rdzun), also brdzun (brdzun) untruthful speech, falsehood, lie, fiction, fable; rdzun-tsig (rdzun tshig), id.; mi-bden rdzún (mi bden rdzun) that is falsehood and not truth Glr.; rdzun-smrá-ba (rdzun smra ba), resp. gsúṅ-ba (gsung ba) B., byéd-pa (byed pa) B., C., *zér-če* (zer ce) W. to lie, rgyál-ba-rnams-kyis rdzun gsúṅ-ba mi srid (rgyal ba rnams kyis rdzun gsung ba mi srid) it is impossible that Buddhas should lie; to tell tales, to make believe, to impose upon; *zun yin* (zun yin) W. you are not in earnest, you only want to quiz me; *zun gyáb-če* (zun gyab ce) W. to lie, to act the hypocrite; *mi še zun gyab* (mi she zun gyab) W. to feign, to pretend ignorance, to disown a person or thing, *mi tsor zun gyab* (mi tshor zun gyab) W. he pretends not to hear it. -- rdzun-_okráb (rdzun 'krab) Sch.: 'an adroit liar and deceiver'. -- rdzún-ma (rdzun ma) 1. = rdzun (rdzun) Dzl. 2. liar Mil. -- *zún-yag-čan* (zun yag can) W. clown, buffoon, merry Andrew.
deceit, imposture Lex., byéd-pa (byed pa) to make a false assertion Tar.; cf. rdzab-rdzub (rdzab rdzub).
something counterfeit, feigned, dissembled, rdzús-mai spráṅ-po (rdzus ma'i sprang po) a disguised beggar Glr.

pf. (b)rdzes ((b)rdzes), fut. brdze (brdze), imp. (b)rdze(s) ((b)rdze(s)) W. *zé ce* (ze ce), 1. to tuck up, truss up, clothes; to cock, a hat; to turn up, the upper-lip Wdṅ.; skra gyén-du brdzés-pa (skra gyen du brdzes pa) the hair bristling Do.; *so or čé-wa zé-če* (so or ce wa ze ce) W. to show one's teeth, to grin. -- 2. to threaten Cs.
dimin. of rdzá-ma (rdza ma), a small pot, pipkin.

1. vb. to be finished, to be at an end, to terminate (Lex. = zín-pa (zin pa)), lam rdzógs-pai mtsáms-su (lam rdzogs pa'i mtshams su) just where the road terminates Mil.; *dá-wa zóg-ne* (da wa zog ne) W. as the month has expired; *i-ru pi-ti yúl-tso zog soṅ* (i ru pi ti yul tsho zog song) W. here the villages of Spiti have an end; mdzád-pa yóṅs-su rdzógs-nas (mdzad pa yongs su rdzogs nas) having accomplished all his deeds Glr.; ji-ltar smón-pa bźín-du yoṅs rdzógs-pas (ji ltar smon pa bzhin du yongs rdzogs pas) all prayers and wishes being fully realized Dzl.; yóṅs-su rdzógs-par tsár-te (yongs su rdzogs par tshar te) when the whole (of the building) was completed Glr. -- 2. adj. perfect, complete, blameless, *gó-lo zog dug* (go lo zog dug) W. the body (of this horse) is without fault; stón-pa dág-par rdzógs-pai saṅs-rgyás (ston pa dag par rdzogs pa'i sangs rgyas) the most perfect teacher, Buddha Glr.; so in a similar manner rdzogs (-pa)-čén(-po) (rdzogs (-pa) -cen (-po)); also yé-šes yóṅs-su rdzógs-pa (ye shes yongs su rdzogs pa) is an appellation of Buddha. -- rdzógs-par (rdzogs par) adv. perfectly, completely, fully (cf. lhúg-par (lhug par)), bsnyád-pa (bsnyad pa) to report circumstantially Dzl., gdams-ṅág gnáṅ-ba (gdams ngag gnang ba) to counsel well Mil.; rdzógs-par šés-pa źig (rdzogs par shes pa zhig) one thoroughly conversant Mil.; rdzógs-par bsláb-pa (rdzogs par bslab pa) to learn thoroughly Mil. -- bsnyén-par rdzógs-pa (bsnyen par rdzogs pa) or bsnyen-rdzógs mdzád-pa (bsnyen rdzogs mdzad pa) to ordain, v. bsnyén-pa (bsnyen pa). -- Comp. rdzogs-ldán (rdzogs ldan) v. dus (dus) 6. -- rdzogs-tsig (rdzogs tshig) v. slár-sdu-ba (slar sdu ba). -- *dzog-yél* (dzog yel) C. obeisance to Chinese officers, in a kneeling posture. -- rdzogsrím (rdzogs rim) v. sub skyéd-pa (skyed pa).

1. (C. vulgo *dzum* (dzum)) castle, fortress; rdzóṅ-dpon (rdzong dpon) lord or governor of a castle, commander of a fortress; *dzoṅ-kyél* (dzong kyel) C., *zoṅ-lén* (zong len) W. letter-post from one nobleman's seat to another. -- 2. the act of accompanying, escorting, _odzoṅs-pa ('dzongs pa) to accompany, to escort Dzl., fee for safe-conduct, travelling-present; dowry, byéd-pa (byed pa) to bestow.

pf. (b)rdzaṅ(s) ((b)rdzang(s)), fut. (b)rdzaṅ ((b)rdzang) to send, to dispatch, presents, ambassadors; to expedite, send off, dismiss; to give to take along with. -- dbugs rdzóṅ-ba (dbugs rdzong ba) shortness of breath, asthma Thgy. and elsewh.
1. vain, empty, spurious, void; kun-rdzób (kun rdzob) v. kun (kun). -- 2. vain, fond of dress W.
